# log-evidence-dr2-suite.txt -- auto-generated by bayes_evidence_suite.py (nlive=500, --dr2); dynesty progress bar -> console only, results captured here
SN likelihood: 1580 SNe (expect 1580), full STAT+SYS covariance
SN data loaded in main process: 1580 SNe (expect 1580)

====================================================================
  NESTED-SAMPLING EVIDENCE — MULTI-MODEL COMPARISON (PHYSICAL BOUND)
  kappa prior: [0, 1]  |  nlive: 500  |  workers: 8
  data: Planck distance priors + DESI DR2 BAO + Pantheon+ (1580 SNe)
====================================================================

Priors (shared: Om in [0.2, 0.45], H0 in [60, 76]; extra DE params below):
  LCDM (w=-1)          k=2   (none)
  wCDM (const w)       k=3   w[-2.5,0]
  RVM (running vac)    k=3   nu[-0.05,0.05]
  Quintessence         k=3   lambda[0,2]
  w0wa (CPL)           k=4   w0[-2.5,0], wa[-4,3]
  Two-Channel          k=5   mu0[0,3], n[0.5,12], kap[0,1]

Executing run 1/6: LCDM (w=-1) (ndim=2) ...
--> LCDM (w=-1): ln(Z) = -712.365 +/- 0.224   chi2min = 1408.22

Executing run 2/6: wCDM (const w) (ndim=3) ...
--> wCDM (const w): ln(Z) = -715.312 +/- 0.269   chi2min = 1406.63

Executing run 3/6: RVM (running vac) (ndim=3) ...
--> RVM (running vac): ln(Z) = -713.891 +/- 0.266   chi2min = 1404.37

Executing run 4/6: Quintessence (ndim=3) ...
--> Quintessence: ln(Z) = -711.458 +/- 0.234   chi2min = 1404.52

Executing run 5/6: w0wa (CPL) (ndim=4) ...
--> w0wa (CPL): ln(Z) = -713.643 +/- 0.290   chi2min = 1399.31

Executing run 6/6: Two-Channel (ndim=5) ...
--> Two-Channel: ln(Z) = -711.342 +/- 0.253   chi2min = 1398.47

==============================================================================
  TABLE 1 — EVIDENCE RANKING
==============================================================================
model                 k   chi2min       ln(Z)    +/-  d lnZ vs best
------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Two-Channel           5   1398.47    -711.342  0.253          0.000  <--best (ref)
Quintessence          3   1404.52    -711.458  0.234         -0.116
LCDM (w=-1)           2   1408.22    -712.365  0.224         -1.023
w0wa (CPL)            4   1399.31    -713.643  0.290         -2.300
RVM (running vac)     3   1404.37    -713.891  0.266         -2.549
wCDM (const w)        3   1406.63    -715.312  0.269         -3.970
------------------------------------------------------------------------------
chi2min = best-fit chi2 (frequentist); ln(Z) = Bayesian evidence, Occam-
penalised for prior volume. d lnZ is measured DOWN from the best model.
Note the two axes can disagree: a lower chi2min can still lose on ln(Z) if
it cost extra parameters/prior volume to get there.

========================================================================
  TABLE 2 — BAYESIAN MODEL SELECTION: TWO-CHANNEL vs EACH RIVAL
  Delta ln Z = ln Z(two-channel) - ln Z(rival)   [+ favours two-channel]
  B = Bayes factor (posterior odds under equal model priors)
========================================================================
rival                   d lnZ    +/-         B  evidence (Jeffreys)         
------------------------------------------------------------------------
LCDM (w=-1)             1.023  0.338      2.78  favours two-channel (positive)
wCDM (const w)          3.970  0.369     52.98  favours two-channel (strong)
RVM (running vac)       2.549  0.368     12.80  favours two-channel (strong)
Quintessence            0.116  0.345      1.12  inconclusive (indistinguishable)
w0wa (CPL)              2.300  0.385      9.98  favours two-channel (positive)
------------------------------------------------------------------------

SUMMARY — Bayesian evidence for the two-channel mechanism (wide kappa prior):
  data favour two-channel over    : LCDM (w=-1), wCDM (const w), RVM (running vac), w0wa (CPL)
  indistinguishable from          : Quintessence
  data disfavour two-channel vs   : none

Jeffreys scale on |Delta ln Z|: < 1 inconclusive; 1-2.5 positive; 2.5-5
strong; > 5 decisive. Being evidentially indistinguishable from the empirical
w0wa form is the intended result -- the mechanism reproduces the evolving-dark-
energy preference at the same fit quality, but from a physical origin for w(z).
========================================================================

PRIOR: kappa in [0, 1] -- the PHYSICAL efficiency bound (headline result).
Cross-check Delta ln Z(two-channel - w0wa) against the first-pass
bayes_evidence.py value of +0.356 (same physical prior).
To run the unphysical stress test, set KAP_MAX = 40.0 at the top and re-run;
the two-channel evidence should DROP by ~ln(40)~3.7 (Occam on impossible volume).
